name: Modal dialog
problem: Users need to complete or acknowledge a focused task without losing the context of the current page.
solution: Present a focused layer over the current interface, constrain interaction to that layer, and provide clear completion and dismissal paths.
problemContext: A user is in a larger page context but needs a short focused task or decision.
Continuing with the background interface before resolving the task would be confusing or unsafe.
selectionRules: Choose a modal only when the user must temporarily leave normal page interaction.
Prefer a full page when the task has many steps, deep content, or durable navigation needs.
Prefer inline disclosure or a popover when the content is optional and does not block progress.
requiredStates: Closed state with a clear invoking control.
Open state with title, focused content, and clear primary and secondary actions.
Dismissed or completed state that returns the user to the originating context.
interactionContract: Opening the modal moves focus inside it and makes the background inert.
Tab and Shift+Tab stay within the modal until it closes.
Escape closes non-destructive modals, and closing restores focus to the invoker.
implementationChecklist: Include a visible title that names the task or decision.
Provide an explicit cancel or close path unless the task truly cannot be dismissed.
Prevent interaction with background controls while the modal is open.
Restore focus to the opener after close.
commonMisuses: Using a modal as a generic content container for routine information.
Opening nested modals or trapping users without a safe exit.
Letting the background page remain interactive.
critiqueQuestions: Does this task truly need to block the rest of the interface, or would inline UI be less disruptive?
useWhen: The task requires focused attention before the user can safely continue.
The content is short enough to complete without navigating away.
avoidWhen: The interaction is informational only and does not require a response.
The task is complex enough to need a full page or persistent workspace.
accessibility: Move focus into the dialog when it opens.
Keep focus within the dialog while it is modal.
Return focus to the invoking control when the dialog closes.
keyboardBehavior: Tab and Shift+Tab cycle through focusable controls inside the dialog.
failureModes: Trapping users without a visible close or cancel path.
Using a modal for routine information that could be inline.

sources.note: Dialog pattern defines modal behavior and keyboard expectations.
Alert guidance distinguishes interruptive modal use from less intrusive alternatives.
